name: Functions use object parameters
instruction: Exported functions with two or more parameters must use a single destructured object parameter instead of positional parameters. This is Payload's backwards-compatibility policy — adding a new option to an object parameter is non-breaking, but adding a positional parameter breaks callers.
relevant_when: TypeScript or TSX file under packages/ that exports a function with two or more parameters (not a test file, not a React component's props which are already an object, not a callback or arrow function passed inline).
context: Payload enforces object parameters for all exported function signatures to maintain backwards compatibility. Positional parameters break the contract silently when signatures change across versions.
scope_reason: Each file's exported function signatures can be inspected independently.

checklist.name: object-params-on-exports
checklist.rule: Every exported function (export function, export const fn =, export default function) that accepts two or more parameters uses a single destructured object parameter ({ param1, param2 }: { param1: Type1, param2: Type2 }) instead of positional parameters (param1: Type1, param2: Type2). Single-parameter functions, React components receiving props, and internal non-exported helpers are exempt.
